texts.content:
From: SecureBank Alerts
To: james.morrison@email.com
Subject: Unusual Activity Detected on Your Account

We noticed a login attempt to your online banking account from an unrecognized device in Jakarta, Indonesia at 3:47 AM EST.

If this was you, no action is needed.
If you did not authorize this login, please reset your password immediately and contact our fraud department at 1-800-555-0199.

For your security, we have temporarily locked access from unknown devices until you verify your identity.

questions.stem: Why did SecureBank send the email to James Morrison?
options: A. To advertise a new savings account
B. To alert him of a suspicious login attempt
C. To confirm his recent wire transfer
D. To request updated contact information
questions.explanation: The email subject and content indicate it was sent because of an unusual login from an unrecognized device.

questions.stem: What can be inferred if James did not log in from Jakarta?
options: A. He should ignore the email
B. His account has been accessed by someone else
C. The bank will close his account automatically
D. He will be charged a fraud protection fee
questions.explanation: If James did not authorize the login, an unauthorized person may have attempted to access his account.

texts.content:
SecureBank – Updated Fee Schedule (Effective March 1)

Monthly maintenance fee: $12 (waived with minimum balance of $1,500)
Overdraft fee: $35 per item (max 4 per day)
ATM fee (non-network): $3 per transaction
Wire transfer (domestic): $15
Wire transfer (international): $35

Paper statements now cost $2 per month. Enroll in e-statements to avoid this charge.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the fee schedule notice?
options: A. To encourage customers to switch banks
B. To inform customers of changes to account fees
C. To explain how to apply for a loan
D. To report a system error in billing
questions.explanation: The notice lists updated fees for various banking services and conditions for waivers.

questions.stem: How can a customer avoid the monthly paper statement fee?
options: A. By maintaining a $1,500 balance
B. By enrolling in e-statements
C. By making fewer than 4 overdrafts per day
D. By using only in-network ATMs
questions.explanation: The notice states to 'enroll in e-statements to avoid this charge.'

questions.stem: What is the maximum total overdraft fee a customer could be charged in one day?
questions.explanation: The overdraft fee is $35 per item with a maximum of 4 per day, so 4 × $35 = $140.
